name: Role @ FactSet
description: Jul 2019 - Apr 2021 · 1 yr 10 mos Norwalk, Connecticut, United States · On-site • Worked on designing a Point-in-Time database that incorporated post-processed company/securities data generated from the raw archived data of past twenty years • Created robust RESTful and GraphQL APIs to make data concurrently accessible to a large number of clients • Worked as the engineer on-call for production related issues on a rotational basis • Developed an internally used product ground-up using .NET, PostgreSQL, GraphQL and Vue.js to maintain thousands of security-linked metadata, to provide a reliable UI for maintaining these records, and to expose the data through APIs serving a large throughput of clients • Worked on creating a new data workflow pipeline using Python (pandas, numpy, sqlalchemy) that improved the runtime of existing ingest/update jobs by more than 20% • Optimized memory footprints of Python workflows dealing with anywhere between 10 - 20 million database rows by half that resulted into significantly cutting the cloud storage cost • Conducted lunch-interviews and partook in interviewer panels for engineering applicants … more University of Connecticut 2 yrs 9 mos Storrs, Connecticut, United States Technical Support Specialist Sep 2017 - May 2019 · 1 yr 9 mos • Designed and maintained the websites for University of Connecticut School of Fine Arts and Benton Museum • Administered the content management system for Benton Museum's record database • Provided IT support to faculties, classrooms, and exhibitions to ensure a reliable technical platform • Trained new hires to get them up-to-speed with the internally used software and technologies … more Research Assistant Sep 2016 - Aug 2018 · 2 yrs I collaborated with Dr. John Ayers on researching better performing growth-platforms for semiconductor devices. In my work, I simulated and analyzed novel growth platforms for InGaAs/GaAs semiconductor devices. I implemented numerous simulation algorithms on MATLAB to analyze chirped and unchirped superlattice structures and quantify their performances. We simulated characteristics such as the average and surface strain, the surface in-plane lattice constant and threading dislocation density profiles for different superlattice structures. From our studies, we showed that - Chirped superlattices can serve as growth-platform with a 7%-46% Indium composition - Compositionally-chirped superlattices allow a wide range of surface in-plane lattice constant for the growth of devices while incorporating alternating local strains to enable dislocation filtering. - Thickness-modulated chirped superlattice can outperform unchirped superlattices and linearly-graded layers as dislocation filters. Based on the research, I authored four research papers that were published in IJHSES CMOC Journal, Journal of the Electrochemical Society (ECS), and International Journal of High Speed Electronics and Systems. I also presented research posters on our findings at the MIT IEEE Undergraduate Research Technology Conference in 2017 and at the Connecticut Optoelectronics and Microelectronics (CMOC) conference in 2017 and 2019. Find my publications on Google Scholar: http://scholar.google.com/citations?user=VLMVgH4AAAAJ … more REU Fellow
